canonical_definition: DCLM is defined as the pre-training data for the DCLM-7B model, with one source also identifying it as a calibration source. Across the provided literature, it is operationalized as an instrument to measure the behavior of language modeling. Researchers use a subset of the DCLM corpus to report a language model's loss, thereby testing its capabilities. This measurement is positioned as a method to "examine how well LMs capture broad and diverse knowledge" and to verify the retention of "diversity and long-tail knowledge coverage." The data is described as a "high-quality corpus carefully curated with complex pipelines and human heuristics."
papers: Beware of Calibration Data for Pruning Large Language Models
Data Selection via Optimal Control for Language Models
MiniPLM: Knowledge Distillation for Pre-training Language Models

definitions.text: The pre-training data of the DCLM-7B model used as a calibration source.
definitions.paper: Beware of Calibration Data for Pruning Large Language Models

relationships.paper: MiniPLM: Knowledge Distillation for Pre-training Language Models
relationships.evidence: We also report the LM’s language modeling loss on a subset of DCLM (Li et al., 2024), a high-quality corpus curated with complex pipelines and human heuristics, to verify that models trained on D′ retain diversity and long-tail knowledge coverage. (Section 3.1)

Data Selection via Optimal Control for Language Models
